topic: Temporal sample construction: lookback, stride, horizon, and alignment
scope: Windowing constructs the learning sample: input-window (lookback) length, stride, prediction horizon, target-window length, input↔target alignment, causal vs. centered windows, overlapping-window dependence, irregular sampling/resampling.
key_concepts: Temporal data cannot be modeled as independent rows; we must construct samples using windowing.
Lookback (input window) determines how much history the model sees per prediction.
Stride determines the step size between consecutive windows, affecting sample overlap and training size.
Prediction horizon and target window define what we are predicting and how far into the future.
Alignment involves matching the input window correctly with the target window to prevent leakage.
Causal windows (past data only) are strictly required for forecasting, whereas centered windows (past and future) can be used for smoothing or imputation but cause leakage if used for prediction.
Irregular sampling requires resampling (downsampling or upsampling) to create fixed intervals before windowing.

definitions.meaning: The process of sliding a fixed-size frame over a time series to extract discrete samples for machine learning.
The length of the historical data window used as input for a single prediction.
The number of time steps the window shifts forward to create the next sample.
The distance in the future from the end of the lookback window to the target being predicted.

examples: Predicting daily sales (horizon = 1 day) using the past 7 days of sales (lookback = 7) shifting by 1 day at a time (stride = 1).

relations.to_chapter_1: Applies the concept of defining the unit of observation (Ch 1 s05) specifically to temporal data.

figures.caption: Konstruksi sampel temporal: Lookback, Horizon, dan Stride
figures.description: Visualisasi bagaimana deret waktu kontinu dipecah menjadi beberapa sampel bersarang dengan jendela input (lookback) dan target (horizon), bergeser sesuai stride.

exclusions: Detailed mathematics of overlapping sample correlation.
